summary: Gate evidence is bound to current HEAD a5203f92 with clean worktree; the reviewer independently re-ran the story unit+e2e suite (21/21 pass) and the typecheck commands (all pass), and the QA bundle artifact focused-a5203f9.json/.log shows 39/39 pass consistent with all three test records. The new judgment_dag_adjudication gate is genuinely wired into the gate DAG — registered in the required-gate whitelist and currently blocking pr-prepare.json (needs_evidence, ready_for_pr_create=false) — and the implementation is fail-closed: stale or head-unbound verdicts count as missing, opt-out requires explicit config, and zero-item routes yield explicit not_applicable. Two low findings: the typecheck record has no machine-readable artifact (honestly disclosed, independently re-verified), and unit/integration/e2e records share one bundle artifact with a minor internal count inconsistency. No fail-open path, stale binding, or misleading green was found.

inspection.summary: Read parallel-dispatch.md, review-request-gate_evidence.md, verification-evidence.json, pr-prepare.json (gate_status/blocking_gates), qa artifacts focused-a5203f9.json/.log; diffed origin/main (src/adjudication.js, src/pr-manager.js, src/cli.js + tests); re-ran node --check on all 3 changed src files and node --test on the 21-test story suite at HEAD a5203f92 (21/21 pass); grep-verified fail-closed head_commit binding and required-gate whitelist registration in collectUnresolvedRequiredGates.
inputs: command: node --check src/adjudication.js src/pr-manager.js src/cli.js -> all pass
git diff origin/main --stat; git rev-parse HEAD (a5203f92, clean)

judgment_delta: Initial concern that one shared bundle artifact (focused-a5203f9.json, 39 tests) backs three records whose commands are subsets was resolved to a low finding after independently re-running the 21-test subset at the same HEAD (passed). Initial concern about typecheck artifact=null was downgraded to low because the record discloses it honestly and the exact node --check commands were re-executed successfully. Initial concern about fail-open paths in the new gate (stale/unbound verdicts, opt-out, empty items) was refuted by code read: unbound/stale verdicts count as missing, opt-out requires explicit enabled:false, zero items produce explicit not_applicable, and pr-prepare.json shows the gate live and blocking.
